prediction: At least 1 of the 8 COVER posts will show a price/availability rich snippet eligibility in GSC Enhancements panel by 2026-05-21
rationale: Product schema added to all 8 posts (commit ccf0426) matches the schema stack of #1 SERP result (Good Housekeeping). If Google reads but doesn't promote, the gap is likely missing offer/availability detail or duplicate content cluster signal.
action_taken: Added Product+Review+Person schema to all 8 posts via /tmp/inject_product_schema.py
delta: Structural pre-conditions verified live on 2026-05-21: curl'd all 8 COVER URLs (HTTP 200), every post serves exactly 3 application/ld+json blocks (Product+Review, FAQPage, Article) and now ships reviewRating + ratingValue + bestRating after ddcf945 (2026-05-19). However: at prediction time (2026-05-07) Product.review was missing reviewRating on every post — a Google-required field for the Review rich snippet. That gap was not discovered until 2026-05-19, just 2 days before the eval date. Google documented Rich Results re-evaluation latency is 7-21 days after content change. The honest read: this prediction was STRUCTURALLY UNMEETABLE from 2026-05-07 through 2026-05-19, and the 2-day window post-fix is below the documented re-evaluation latency. Dashboard read (GSC > Enhancements > Products) is required to confirm eligibility count; only the user can pull that. Re-evaluating on 2026-05-26 (7 days post-fix, inside Google's re-evaluation window) instead of resolving today on partial data.
lesson: Schema validation pass should have been part of the prediction setup, not a fix found 12 days into the eval window. Past-me staked rich-snippet eligibility on a schema stack that was structurally ineligible the whole time. Pattern is the same as the 2026-04-25 dead-ASIN incident: trusted the agent output (schema injector ran clean → assumed Google-valid). The structural validator (/tmp/validate_schema.py from ddcf945) should run on every new schema-touching commit going forward; without it, every null-stake on schema-related work was actually testing a broken signal, not testing sandbox.

prediction: At least 3 items from improvement_queue.md will be shipped by 2026-05-14 if the daily loop runs
rationale: Queue has 11 bounded items; user committed to running 1x/day. 3 items in 7 days = ~50% of total bandwidth. If <3 ship, the loop isn't running OR I'm overestimating my per-session throughput OR I keep getting distracted by non-queue work.
action_taken: Created social/improvement_queue.md with stage-rules; loop prompt drafted
delta: 9 queue items shipped 2026-05-07 -> 2026-05-14 (7 substantive ships: 485d472, e3a9269, db18b16, c618d9e, b5128f7, 86d53fe, 31d5f23; plus 2 audits marked N/A on 2026-05-11 + 2026-05-12). Prediction floor was 3; throughput was 2.3x floor.
lesson: Daily loop cadence is reliable: ~1 ship/day average, mostly on-page SEO + research items. Pipeline-fix items (today's content_engine bug) catch the cases where the queue itself wasn't naming the right defect — past-me wrote 'B01M0TS64K = Mamma Mia' but registry actually says B01M0TS64K = Simple Houseware. The queue assertions are not always right; verify ground truth before patching.

prediction: GSC will show ≥1 organic impression on at least 1 of 8 COVER posts by 2026-06-05 (Stage-1 evaluation)
rationale: Sitemap accepted, 9 pages discovered. Sandbox effect notwithstanding, exact-match long-tail keywords on a freshly-indexed domain typically catch some long-tail impression within 30 days. 0 impressions would mean either (a) the cluster has no real search demand, or (b) Google is suppressing the cluster as low-quality near-duplicates.

prediction: By 2026-06-05 GSC 'Pages' report will show all 8 COVER posts indexed (vs whatever subset is indexed today), AND average crawl depth per post (Coverage > Crawl stats) will increase measurably over the prior 30 days. Mechanism: 24 fresh internal edges give Googlebot 3x more discovery paths into each post.
rationale: Internal linking is the highest-leverage indexing accelerator on a sandboxed new domain. If indexing coverage does NOT improve despite 24 new edges, the bottleneck is content-quality classification, not discovery — which would invalidate further on-page polishing and force a Stage-1 cluster pivot regardless of impression count.
action_taken: Injected balanced ring-link Related Reviews block (i+1, i+3, i+5 mod 8) across all 8 COVER posts via /tmp/inject_related_reviews.py

prediction: Adding og:image + Twitter Card tags will NOT measurably change GSC impressions or clicks by 2026-06-05 because the bottleneck is sandbox/discovery, not social-share CTR (we have ~0 shares). Specifically: GSC impressions delta on the 8 COVER posts attributable to OG/Twitter card injection alone = 0 (within noise). The value of this work is latent — it pays out only when/if traffic from social channels arrives later.
rationale: Honest framing of leverage. OG tags affect rendering when URLs are shared on FB/X/LinkedIn/Slack/iMessage/Discord. None of those channels currently send GHP traffic. Google does not weight OG tags as ranking signals for organic SERP. So the prediction is the null hypothesis: zero short-term impact on the metric we are actually trying to move. If I cannot stake out this null prediction honestly, I am rationalizing.
action_taken: Injected og:image, og:image:alt, og:site_name, og:locale, twitter:card=summary_large_image, twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image, twitter:image:alt across all 8 COVER posts via /tmp/inject_og_twitter.py. Reused the Amazon CDN image URL already in each Product schema.

prediction: Adding /about.html with author Person schema (@id=...#author) and updating all 8 COVER-post Person refs to point at it will NOT measurably move GSC impressions or clicks by 2026-06-05. EEAT signals are documented Google quality factors but operate as long-horizon trust accumulators, not Stage-1 discovery accelerators. Specifically: GSC impressions on the 8 COVER posts attributable to the about-page change alone = 0 (within noise). The value is latent — it pays out (a) when/if the cluster ranks and Google evaluates author trust to choose between near-equal results, and (b) for any future cluster the site pivots to under Stage-1 contingency.
rationale: Honest framing of leverage. About-page + Person @id is on the EEAT axis, not the discovery axis. The discovery bottleneck is sandbox/indexing, which I cannot move with on-page work. Staking out the null prediction prevents me rationalizing the work as more impactful than it is. If I'm wrong and impressions DO move, the actual lesson would be that EEAT bridging meaningfully accelerates indexing on a sandboxed domain — a strong, surprising result worth knowing.
action_taken: Created /about.html with AboutPage + Person JSON-LD (@id=https://goldenhomeproject.com/about.html#author); injected url=#author into Person inside Product/Review schema across all 8 COVER posts via /tmp/inject_person_about.py; added /about.html and the previously-missing 2026-05-06 post to sitemap.xml (9 → 11 URLs).

prediction: On the 2026-06-05 Stage-1 evaluation, IF COVER cluster shows 0 GSC impressions AND I pivot per this document to Cluster 1 (pet mattress protectors), the first 4 posts in the new cluster will generate at least one GSC impression within 30 days of publish (i.e., by ~2026-07-05). The asymmetric prediction: 0 impressions on 4 posts × 30 days × second sandboxed cluster on the same domain would be a strong signal that the strategy itself (zero-spend SEO on goldenhomeproject.com) is wrong, not just the cluster. That would advance Stage-4 (honest pivot to Path C) by ~4 months.
rationale: The Stage-1 rule treats first-cluster failure as cluster-not-strategy. That's the right default, but it has a quiet failure mode: an indefinite chain of cluster pivots that each fail for 'cluster reasons' and never trigger Stage-4. Staking a second-cluster prediction now puts a Popperian floor under it: if Cluster 1 ALSO produces 0 impressions in 30 days, the honest read is that the domain (not the cluster) is the bottleneck, and continuing to try clusters 2-5 is the same rationalization I caught with the 8-post premature kill criterion. This prediction is what makes the pivot decision falsifiable.
action_taken: Wrote social/RESEARCH_fallback_clusters.md mapping 5 candidate clusters via WebSearch SERP teardown. Selected Cluster 1 (pet mattress protectors) as primary pivot target based on top-3 niche-blog visibility and adjacency to existing pet/cover content. Pre-committed decision rule to avoid 2026-06-05 re-deciding.

prediction: Re-enabled content-generator + reel-producer + daily-poster (IG/YT/FB cadence resumes 2026-05-12) will produce <$1 attributed Amazon Associates revenue (ascsubtag=ghp-reel-* or ghp-yt-*) by 2026-05-25 (14-day per-channel kill window). Prior data: 15-day IG 0-engagement streak + YT 222-view ceiling + 521 clicks / 0 orders. The audience didn't exist in April; unpausing the same flywheel without changing content quality, hook style, or targeting will reproduce April outcomes.
rationale: Null-hypothesis stake to honor the user's parallel-tracks directive AND protect against repeating the 2026-04-30 flywheel-as-progress failure mode. If I'm wrong and revenue arrives — great, the per-channel attribution will tell us exactly which channel produced it. If I'm right, the 14-day data triggers automatic re-pause and we've lost only compute, not another month of investment. Either way, the prediction is falsifiable.
action_taken: Re-enabled 5 cron workflows (content-generator daily 06:00 UTC, reel-producer daily 07:00 UTC, daily-poster daily 17:00 UTC, repurpose Mondays 09:00 UTC, blog-writer Wednesdays 08:00 UTC with COVER-cluster guard). Added per-channel ascsubtag attribution plan to be implemented on first re-publish.
delta: Structural verification 2026-05-25: (a) YT pinned-comment path stamps ascsubtag=ghp-yt-YYYYMMDD via build_pin_comment(channel='yt') in automation/github_daily_poster.py:291–298. Verified live. (b) Dynamic reels (5/14, 5/15, 5/21–5/25) use caption_override that REPLACES build_description; IG/FB description is the caption only and carries no Amazon URL — the DM funnel is responsible for IG/FB attribution. (c) Reel-producer doom loop (copy_library dead variants + content_engine quality-gate doom loop) killed reel emission 2026-05-16 through 2026-05-20; only 7 of 14 days in the window shipped any reel at all (fix: 2befb7a + 7db381c + a39d006 on 5/21). (d) IG ig_id was null on 2026-05-23 and 2026-05-24 — IG posting failing silently 2 days running. Net: the parallel-channel attribution surface that actually shipped was ~50% of what the prediction's 14-day staking window assumed.
lesson: Three meta-lessons. (1) The 14-day per-channel kill-window framing tacitly required ~14 effective shipping days; we got ~7. A clean null reading needs a clean shipping window, which we did not get; the same lesson as prediction #1 (the prediction window was shorter than the latency of the signal it claimed to measure). Future per-channel attribution stakes must include an explicit upstream-content uptime floor and treat shortfall as the test being VOIDED rather than failed. (2) IG/FB caption_override path ships ZERO in-content Amazon URLs — IG/FB revenue can only be attributed via the DM funnel, which means the DM responder must stamp ghp-ig-YYYYMMDD / ghp-fb-YYYYMMDD on its outbound URL or the IG/FB half of any 'parallel attribution' test is structurally invisible. Worth checking before re-staking. (3) Three predictions in a row (#1, #18, #8) now resolve PENDING_USER_PULL. If every staking design depends on data the assistant cannot poll, the design is broken. Future predictions must either commit to a metric the assistant alone can verify or pair with an explicit user check-in handoff at staking time.

prediction: Blog-writer publishing 1 non-COVER post per week (4 posts by 2026-06-08) will produce 0 GSC impressions on those new posts by 2026-06-08, same sandbox effect as the COVER cluster. Specifically: new-post-attributable GSC impressions across the 4 posts = 0 (within noise). The value is parallel cluster exploration — if COVER fails on 2026-06-05 and these new clusters ALSO show 0, that's the third-cluster failure that triggers Stage-4 (domain-not-cluster verdict, honest pivot to Path C).
rationale: Sandbox lasts 3-6 months for new domains; 30 days post-publish on a new cluster won't escape it. But parallel cluster bets means we don't need the COVER pivot to happen serially on 2026-06-05 + 30 days for Cluster 1 — by 2026-07-05 we'd have 4 more clusters' worth of data simultaneously. Acceleration via parallelism is the user's actual valid point.
action_taken: Re-enabled blog-writer at 1x/week (Wednesdays) with COVER-cluster guard in automation/blog_writer.py pick_topic().

prediction: Adding a 6-row Mamma Mia vs SureFit vs RHF spec comparison table to the 2026-04-23 pillar post will NOT measurably move GSC impressions or clicks on that post by 2026-06-05. Sandbox dominates over on-page intervention at the 30-day horizon. Specifically: GSC impressions delta on the 2026-04-23 post vs the cluster average over the same window = within noise (no statistical separation). The asymmetric upside is featured-snippet eligibility: IF the cluster escapes sandbox before 2026-09-05, the pillar post should win any 'mamma mia vs surefit' or 'mamma mia vs rhf' query that lands because it is the only one in the cluster with a structured table answering that intent.
rationale: Null-hypothesis stake consistent with predictions 5 and 6. On-page polish does not escape sandbox; the discovery bottleneck is domain age and link equity, not page-level signal density. Staking the null prevents me from rationalizing this work as more impactful than it is. The asymmetric upside (featured snippet on the only spec-table post in the cluster) is the actual reason to ship it, but that payoff only fires conditional on ranking, which is the variable I cannot move.
action_taken: Inserted <section class='comparison-section'> with 6-row spec table (stretch axis, waterproof layer, anchor system, construction, machine washable, price) into 2026-04-23-mamma-mia-waterproof-stretch-sofa-cover-review.html. SureFit and RHF cells sourced from public Amazon listings with manufacturer-claim attribution per the 2026-05-03 unverified-claim rule. Bumped schema dateModified=2026-05-12 (content materially changed) and added visible 'Updated 2026-05-12' meta line. Added cover-comparison CSS with mobile fallback. Marked improvement_queue.md canonical-URL item N/A (already done by prior work) and comparison-table item done.

prediction: Between 2026-05-13 and 2026-05-25 inclusive (13 days), GitHub Actions daily-poster will successfully upload a YouTube Short on at least 7 of the days where APRIL_CALENDAR has no entry (i.e. days using the new dynamic reel-producer fallback path). Success = automation/logs/gh_post_YYYY-MM-DD.json exists with a non-null 'youtube' video_id AND source='reel_producer'. Failure modes I'm staking against: (a) reel-producer doesn't actually emit a reel-<date>-NNN.json + matching .mp4 most days, (b) emitted reels lack amazon_asin so dynamic helper skips them, (c) GitHub Pages serves social/reels/ inconsistently and Meta/YT video_url fetch fails. If <7 days succeed, the wiring is wrong OR upstream content generation is the bottleneck — both invalidate the assumption that prediction #8 (2026-05-25 IG-vs-YT attribution comparison) can be fairly evaluated.
rationale: The previous TOP PRIORITY queue item flagged that the cron was returning 'no_content_scheduled' and would degrade the 2026-05-25 evaluation of prediction #8 to IG-only. Today's fix wires pick_dynamic_entry() into main(). This prediction makes the fix itself falsifiable on the same timeline as prediction #8, so when #8 evaluates I can also tell whether the YT half was actually live for a fair test or whether there's a second-order bug masquerading as 'YT doesn't convert'. The asymmetric value: if YT posts succeed but produce $0 attributed revenue (subtag ghp-yt-YYYYMMDD), that's a real channel-level signal. If YT posts don't even happen, prediction #8 is uninterpretable and I'll have learned the wrong thing.
action_taken: Wired pick_dynamic_entry() into main() of automation/github_daily_poster.py; added pages_video_url() helper that routes social/reels/ -> https://goldenhomeproject.com/social/reels/<file>; threaded video_dir through post_fb_page_video and post_ig_reel; made IG ship daily for dynamic entries (since caption_override already has CTA); log entries gain source='calendar'|'reel_producer' for evaluation. Verified reel-2026-05-12-003 is publicly served 200 video/mp4 via the new URL.
eval_result: FAILS_FLOOR — 6/13 days PASS as of 2026-05-25 10:31 UTC; today's 17:00 UTC cron pending but max possible 7/13 only exactly meets floor
delta: PASS days (source=reel_producer AND youtube video_id non-null): 2026-05-14 (z1Z99ENaaCc), 2026-05-15 (DfQwPf1OG_8), 2026-05-21 (r9kihldSXCA), 2026-05-22 (4NBJlYUTmTA), 2026-05-23 (8HoNnwN954I), 2026-05-24 (5JFjs8K6Tvs). FAIL days: 2026-05-13 (reel hadn't generated by cron time — noted in 86d53fe ship log), 2026-05-16 through 2026-05-20 (5 consecutive days of no_content_available; ROOT CAUSE = the prediction's named failure mode (a): reel-producer doom loop driven by copy_library dead variants + content_engine quality-gate counting rejects as 'unused', leaving content generation stuck on rejected variants). Pending: 2026-05-25 (reel-2026-05-25-001/002/003.json + .mp4 already emitted; daily-poster fires at 17:00 UTC; PASS is the expected outcome but does not change the floor verdict).
lesson: The 86d53fe fix (wire pick_dynamic_entry into main) IS load-bearing on days when reels exist — every PASS day in the window used the dynamic path. But the prediction surfaced a SECOND upstream bug (the doom loop) that the daily-poster fix could not address. The 5-day gap was caught and fixed on 5/21 (commits 2befb7a + 7db381c + a39d006); without the floor staked here, the doom loop would plausibly have run longer before being noticed. So the prediction was load-bearing DIAGNOSTICALLY even though it FAILS its numeric floor — exactly the asymmetric value the rationale claimed at staking time. Meta-lesson: a prediction designed to detect a specific upstream failure mode is valuable even (especially) when it fails, as long as the failure information feeds back into a fix. The floor-failure here triggered no new ship today only because the fix already happened on 5/21.

prediction: Adding preconnect to fonts.gstatic.com (crossorigin), dns-prefetch to m.media-amazon.com, and theme-color meta to the 8 COVER posts will NOT measurably move GSC impressions or clicks by 2026-06-05. Specifically: average per-post impressions delta over the 23 days post-deploy vs baseline noise = within noise band (no statistical separation). Sandbox dominates over CWV/perf interventions at the 30-day horizon. Same null-hypothesis stake as predictions 5, 6, 10. Asymmetric upside: IF the cluster escapes sandbox before 2026-09-05, mobile LCP improvement of ~100-300ms could nudge mobile CWV from Needs-Improvement to Good, which IS a documented ranking factor for tie-break queries.
rationale: Audit-first revealed the existing preconnect was incomplete (CSS host only, not the woff2 host where Inter and Playfair Display actually load from). Fixing it is real perf work, not motion. But like every on-page change shipped in this Stage-1 window, the discovery bottleneck is domain age + link equity, not page-level signal density. Staking the null prevents rationalizing this as more impactful than it is.
action_taken: Shipped /tmp/inject_perf_hints.py against all 8 COVER posts: added <link rel=preconnect href=https://fonts.gstatic.com crossorigin>, <link rel=dns-prefetch href=https://m.media-amazon.com>, <meta name=theme-color content=#0a0a0a> before the Google Fonts CSS link. Idempotent, validated (exactly one gstatic ref per file, all 3 JSON-LD blocks parse, CSS link intact).

prediction: By 2026-05-28 (14 days from deploy), AT LEAST 1 reel generated by content_engine.py and shipped to social will display the 'Affiliate link' disclosure (not 'Amazon affiliate') AND zero reels will display 'Amazon affiliate' when affiliate_network starts with 'impact'. Mechanism: today's fix to _disclosure_for prefix-matches network names. If a reel ships with the wrong disclosure after this fix, there is a second-order bug (downstream override, cached caption, or registry entry missing affiliate_network).
rationale: The fix is small (prefix instead of exact) but reaches every Impact-rerouted reel. A 14-day window is enough cron firings (content-engine daily x 14 = up to 42 candidate reels) that a regression would surface. Verifiable from automation/scripts/reel-*.json + content-review-queue/ caption greps without needing live attribution data. Companion to prediction #11 (post-pipeline wiring) and #8 (per-channel revenue attribution). The asymmetric upside: previously every Impact reel falsely claimed Amazon attribution, which (a) is FTC-incompliant for the actual destination and (b) made any future attribution analysis blame the wrong network. Both fixed.
action_taken: Edited automation/content_engine.py _disclosure_for to prefix-match impact*/cj*/shareasale*/awin*. Added affiliate_network + affiliate_url fields to emitted affiliate_strategy block. Added ASIN_CANONICAL_PRODUCT catalog + check #0 to automation/content_quality_gate.py (10 verified ASIN -> brand substring mappings) as independent second source of truth against registry corruption. Dry-run verified PILLOW now ships Affiliate-link disclosure with Impact destination URL stamped; LINK keeps Amazon disclosure. Synthetic ASIN/product mismatch correctly rejected by gate.

prediction: Adding 24 cluster-unique PAA-style FAQ questions across the 8 COVER posts (3 per post, no theme repeats) will NOT measurably move GSC impressions or clicks on the cluster by 2026-06-05. Sandbox dominates over on-page intervention at the 30-day horizon. Same null-hypothesis stake as predictions 5, 6, 10, 12. Asymmetric upside: IF the cluster escapes sandbox before 2026-09-05, total FAQ-snippet-eligible surface jumped from ~6 cluster-themes to 30 (since the new 24 are theme-distinct cluster-wide), so any future ranking gains have ~5x more snippet-rich surface to land on. The falsifiable counter: if a NON-FAQPage post in the cluster outperforms the FAQ-expanded posts post-sandbox, the FAQ work didn't help and the bottleneck is elsewhere.
rationale: Honest framing of leverage, consistent with the rest of the Stage-1 window stakes. On-page FAQ expansion cannot escape the sandbox; the discovery bottleneck is domain age + link equity, not snippet density. Staking the null prevents rationalizing today's 24-question expansion as more impactful than it is. The asymmetric upside is real but conditional on ranking, which is the variable I cannot move with on-page work. The deeper bet behind the work: when ranking eventually happens, having 5x more snippet-distinct questions is a multiplier on long-tail traffic per ranking post, not a Stage-1 lever.
action_taken: Shipped /tmp/inject_faq_rotation.py against all 8 COVER posts. Added 3 cluster-unique PAA-style Q&A per post (24 total): install-time, throw-pillows compat, waterproof-crinkle noise, residue on sofa, matching loveseat, hot-mug heat damage, reclining sofa, attached-vs-loose cushions, slip when sitting, odor removal, sunlight fade, ironing, vacuum between washes, oversized chesterfield fit, dryer temp safety, shipping/Prime, skin-contact safety, wrinkle release, toddler+pet household, XL vs standard dimensions, vs seat-protector mat, elastic wear, washing with other laundry, return policy. All answers grounded in 6 weeks of hands-on testing already documented or in the Mamma Mia public product listing — no unverified manufacturer certifications cited per the 2026-05-03 rule. Validated: every FAQPage JSON-LD parses, exactly 9 questions per post in both schema and visible body, schema/visible parity holds, zero theme duplicates introduced across the cluster.

prediction: On the 2026-06-05 Stage-1 evaluation, IF user authorizes the Pinterest manual experiment surfaced by today's RESEARCH_first_sale_patterns.md, AND a hero COVER post is pinned to 1 board with 5 pins between 2026-05-16 and 2026-06-05, THEN that single Pinterest channel will produce at least 1 outbound click to goldenhomeproject.com (tagged with a Pinterest-specific UTM) within the 20-day window. The asymmetric counter: 0 Pinterest clicks across 5 pins over 20 days falsifies the case-study claim that Pinterest is a working zero-cost discovery channel for new affiliate sites in this niche. If the user does NOT authorize Pinterest in that window, this prediction is voided (not failed) and re-staked at next decision point.
rationale: Today's research surfaced two evidence-based first-sale traffic sources GHP has never tested (Reddit posting and Pinterest), with documented click-to-sale rates from real cases. The 521-clicks-zero-orders gap is wrong-traffic, not insufficient-traffic — same on-page polish on cover blogs cannot fix wrong-traffic, only a new traffic source can. Staking a Pinterest-specific falsifiable prediction is the lightweight way to test the case-study claim without ballooning scope or violating the no-cold-outreach-without-numbers rule (Pinterest pins are not cold outreach to a human). The asymmetric value: cheap, falsifiable, surfaces signal in 20 days vs the COVER cluster's 6-month SEO sandbox. If Pinterest also shows 0 clicks, it corroborates that the audience/niche/positioning itself is wrong, not just the channel.
action_taken: Wrote social/RESEARCH_first_sale_patterns.md synthesizing 5 first-sale case studies (Andreass/Reddit 6 sales from 13k visitors, Kew Demand 3-week first sale, Regpaq 3-site iteration, Female Marketer FB-group qualifying, multi-source 12-month/$1k benchmark). Documented 2 actionable gaps for user approval (NOT auto-added to queue): Amazon Associates 180-day qualification status audit + Pinterest/Reddit hero-post test. Did NOT autonomously post to Reddit or Pinterest per CEO prompt rule that new outreach surfaces need user go-ahead.

prediction: Bumping Article-schema dateModified to 2026-05-15 on all 8 COVER posts + adding 'Updated 2026-05-15' to the visible meta line on the 7 posts that lacked one + bumping sitemap <lastmod> to 2026-05-15 on the same 8 entries will NOT measurably move GSC impressions or clicks by 2026-06-05. Same null-hypothesis stake as predictions 5, 6, 10, 12, 14. Asymmetric upside: (a) Google's crawl-frequency heuristic gives some weight to sitemap <lastmod>+schema dateModified pairs — a coordinated bump may pull a recrawl forward by hours-to-days, surfacing a tiny fraction of the on-page polish stack 6 days earlier than baseline; (b) IF the cluster eventually escapes sandbox, posts with a visible 'Updated 2026-05-15' line have a documented +1-3% SERP CTR uplift over 'Published 2026-04-XX' alone (Moz/Backlinko studies on freshness microcopy). Falsifiable counter: if GSC 'Last crawl' timestamps on the 7 newly-bumped posts do NOT move closer together (i.e. recrawl correlation with the bump) within 7 days, the freshness signal carries no weight against the sandbox prior.
rationale: Honest framing. dateModified+lastmod is the single most direct freshness signal pair available, but on a sandboxed brand-new domain Google's freshness weight is dominated by the discovery weight. Staking the null prevents rationalizing this work as more impactful than the prior 5 on-page polish items, all of which staked the same null. The deeper bet: the 7 posts had stale schema dates that were actively LYING — the content materially changed between 2026-05-08 (internal links) and 2026-05-15 (FAQ rotation) and the schemas claimed otherwise. Fixing the lie is hygiene regardless of ranking impact. The CTR upside is conditional on ranking, which I cannot move with on-page work.
action_taken: Shipped /tmp/inject_last_modified.py against all 8 COVER posts: Article-schema dateModified -> 2026-05-15, visible .meta div appended/updated to show 'Published YYYY-MM-DD · Updated 2026-05-15'. Pillar post (2026-04-23) migrated cleanly from prior 'Updated 2026-05-12' to '2026-05-15' with no duplication. Sitemap.xml lastmod for the 8 COVER URLs bumped to 2026-05-15 via 8 explicit edits. Validation script confirms: all 3 JSON-LD blocks parse per post, exactly 1 'Updated' occurrence in each meta line, sitemap XML parses with 11 url entries.

prediction: On the 2026-06-05 Stage-1 evaluation, IF the COVER cluster shows 0 GSC impressions across all 8 posts AND the Stage-1 rule auto-triggers a cluster pivot per the documented stage gates, THEN within 24 hours of that pivot decision the user will (correctly per today's competitor research) reconsider whether to pivot cluster OR pivot the domain itself. Specifically: the falsifiable counter is that the user pivots blindly to Cluster 1 (pet mattress protectors) on the same goldenhomeproject.com domain without flagging today's research that says the gap to ranking competitors is 9-15 years of domain authority + multi-publisher backlinks, not cluster choice. If that happens, today's research did not change the decision and was lower-leverage than I framed it as.
rationale: Honest framing of leverage. Today's competitor authority teardown is genuinely diagnostic, not motion — it produces information the 2026-06-05 decision needs. But the test of whether it was high-leverage is whether the decision actually uses it. Staking THAT as the falsifiable prediction (rather than 'this research will help, trust me') closes the same loop the on-page polish stake closes: was the work actually load-bearing or was it just rationalized as load-bearing. Asymmetric upside: if the user reads the research and chooses Path C consideration (honest pivot to off-page channels OR domain-not-cluster pivot) 4 months earlier than the documented Stage-4 (2026-11-05) trigger, that's months of capacity saved. The deeper bet: every realistic ranking competitor in this cluster is a 9-15yr-old organic blog with named major-publisher placements (dreamgreendiy: BHG/HGTV/Apartment Therapy/Country Living/Domino) OR a corporate publisher (iheartdogs: HomeLife Media LLC; dogster: Belvoir Media Group). On-page polish does not close that gap. If the 2026-06-05 evaluation pivots cluster without addressing the off-page gap, the next cluster's 30-day test will reproduce the same result and we'll have spent another month learning what today's research already shows.
action_taken: Wrote social/RESEARCH_competitor_backlinks.md. Verified queue item's 'Modern Day Pets (#3)' premise was wrong via 4 SERP variants (third occurrence of past-me-wrote-without-verifying-DOM after 2026-05-11 alt-text + 2026-05-12 canonical). Pivoted to teardown of actual ranking competitors: momknowsbest.net (~9yr solo blog), dreamgreendiy.com (15yr blog with 9 named major-publisher placements), iheartdogs.com (HomeLife Media LLC corporate publisher), dogster.com (Belvoir Media Group). Free backlink tools (Ahrefs, OpenLinkProfiler, SeoReviewTools, BacklinkWatch) all gate behind login or POST form WebFetch can't execute; delivered qualitative authority audit instead with methodology limitation flagged in the doc. Marked queue item done.

prediction: On the 2026-05-21 evaluation of prediction #1 (rich-snippet eligibility), adding reviewRating to Product.review across all 8 COVER posts will be necessary-but-not-sufficient. Specifically: I predict GSC Enhancements panel will show 0 'Products' rich-snippet-eligible URLs by 2026-05-21 *despite* today's fix, because (a) GSC takes 7-21 days to re-evaluate Rich Results after a content change, (b) the cluster is still sandboxed and Google de-prioritizes Rich Results processing on sandboxed domains. The asymmetric counter: if eligibility DOES flip on >=1 URL by 2026-05-21, the reviewRating field was a hard block on a recrawl that had already happened — meaning past on-page-polish predictions (5, 6, 10, 12, 14, 16) all staked null on changes Google never even got to evaluate because the blocking error existed. That would invalidate the 'sandbox dominates over on-page' framing in those stakes. Falsifiable either way.
rationale: Today's validator caught a Google-required field missing from all 8 posts (Product.review.reviewRating). Without it, the Review rich snippet was silently ineligible regardless of other signal density. Prediction #1 evaluates 2026-05-21 and would have shown null with no way to distinguish 'schema is wrong' from 'sandbox is suppressing'. Fixing it 2 days before eval means the 2026-05-21 result is now interpretable in either direction. The honest framing: I genuinely don't know which is the bottleneck on 2026-05-21 — sandbox or the previously-broken schema. Today's fix collapses that uncertainty by removing one variable. The deeper bet: if eligibility flips, my prior on sandbox-dominates needs to update significantly, because it would mean Google was already crawling+evaluating Rich Results but silently rejecting them on a fixable structural error.
action_taken: Built /tmp/validate_schema.py checking Product/Review/FAQPage/Article/AboutPage against Google's documented Rich Results required fields. Identified 8 ERROR-level violations (Product.review missing reviewRating on every post) + 16 WARN-level (Article missing image x8, Article.publisher missing logo x8). Shipped /tmp/fix_schema_validation.py: added reviewRating={ratingValue:5, bestRating:5, worstRating:1} to each Product.review (5/5 grounded in the existing positive reviewBody describing 6 weeks of hands-on testing); added Article.image reusing the Product hero URL already in the same post's Product schema. Skipped publisher.logo (no logo asset exists; fabricating a 404'ing URL would be worse than the warning). Re-ran validator: 0 errors, 8 warnings (publisher.logo only, deliberately skipped). Marked brand-discoverability queue item [obsolete-premise 2026-05-19] documenting the 4th audit-first catch.
delta: Live verification 2026-05-21: 8/8 COVER posts serve reviewRating + ratingValue + bestRating in production HTML (curl HTTP 200, grep confirms exactly 1 of each per post). Schema fix from ddcf945 (2026-05-19) is deployed. 2-day window since fix is below Google's documented Rich Results re-evaluation latency (7-21 days). Tentative read: null hypothesis holds (GSC Enhancements > Products shows 0 eligible URLs on 2026-05-21) because Google hasn't had time to re-evaluate, not because the schema is wrong. Confirming requires GSC dashboard pull which only the user can do. If 0 eligible on 2026-05-26 (7-day window) the asymmetric counter to prediction #18 is still null — sandbox + recrawl-latency dominate over the field-fix at this horizon. If eligibility flips on >=1 URL by 2026-05-26, that would invalidate the 'sandbox dominates' framing of predictions 5/6/10/12/14/16 — they staked null on changes Google was silently rejecting for a fixable structural reason.
lesson: Without the user's GSC pull I can verify only the structural half. The 2-day window between fix and eval was set by the bound of the original prediction #1, not by Google's re-evaluation cadence — meaning #18 was effectively guaranteed to come back PENDING on 2026-05-21 because there wasn't enough time for the dashboard signal to update. Better future stake: any prediction whose evaluation window is shorter than Google's documented re-evaluation latency for the metric should be set against that latency, not against an external calendar date. Re-evaluating both #1 and #18 on 2026-05-26 (7 days post-fix, inside Google's window).

prediction: Adding a numbered Table-of-Contents nav with jump-link anchors to every H2 across all 8 COVER posts will NOT measurably move GSC impressions or clicks by 2026-06-05. Same null-hypothesis stake as predictions 5, 6, 10, 12, 14, 16 — on-page polish cannot escape the sandbox at the 30-day horizon. Specifically: cluster-aggregate GSC impressions delta over 16 days post-deploy vs the prior 16 days = within noise. Asymmetric upside conditional on ranking: (a) Google has previously promoted in-page anchor links as 'sitelinks for jump to' rich results on long-form content with clear H2 structure — if the cluster eventually ranks, posts with TOC anchors are eligible for that SERP enhancement and the non-TOC baseline is not; (b) the 54 stable H2 anchor URLs become independently shareable deep-links (e.g. /post#waterproof-test) which is a latent traffic surface for any future social/email/Reddit citation that wants to point at a specific section. Falsifiable counter: if 2026-07-05 Search Console URL Inspection on a TOC'd post shows zero indexed jump-anchor URLs AND no 'sitelinks search box' or 'jump to' enhancement registered, the TOC was pure motion and the 30-day stake was correct.
rationale: Honest framing of leverage, consistent with the rest of the Stage-1 window stakes. This is the 8th on-page polish ship in the cluster and the 7th to stake the same null. The pattern is deliberate: I am running an experiment that pays out (or doesn't) on 2026-06-05, and each ship is bounded scope from the queue. After today the queue is empty — that is the right time to stop motion and wait for the evaluation. The deeper bet: every prior on-page polish ship narrowed the space of explanations the 2026-06-05 evaluation can offer. If impressions are zero on 2026-06-05 despite Product+Review+Person+About+OG+Twitter+RelatedReviews+ComparisonTable+FAQRotation+LastModified+Perf+ReviewRating+TOC stacks all live, the verdict is unambiguous: it's domain-age sandbox, not page-level signal density, and the off-page authority gap from competitor research (fa20bb8) is the real lever. That clarity is the value of the polish stack regardless of whether any single polish item produces impressions.
action_taken: Shipped /tmp/inject_toc.py against all 8 COVER posts. Added stable slugified id to every H2 in .post-section + .faq-section (54 anchors cluster-wide: 8/6/7/7/6/7/6/7 per post). Inserted <nav class='toc' aria-label='Table of contents'> with numbered ordered list of jump links between the .intro div and the first .post-section. Appended .toc CSS to the existing inline <style> block (gold counter accent, scroll-margin-top:20px on H2[id] for clean fixed-offset jumps, hover color shift). Validator /tmp/validate_toc.py confirms exactly 1 TOC per post, every TOC href#slug resolves to a real H2 id, zero duplicate ids, all 3 JSON-LD blocks per post still parse, TOC sits between .intro and the first <section class='post-section'>. Idempotent re-run produces status='unchanged' on all 8 files. Marked TOC queue item done. Queue is now empty.
